titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 66569

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=614 status=live modified=2017-08-23 15:26:07.906463+00 | translation moa=615 status=live modified=2017-07-20 09:56:38.271015+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus hoc scriptum visuris vel audituris . Patricius filius Willelmi eternam in domino salutem . Nouerit
vniuersitas vestra dominum abbatem et Conuentum de Scon’ michi de omnimodis debitis satisfecisse . in quibus mihi tam per
scripta quam per aliquem alium contractum super instauramento et firmis de Blar’ . et omnibus aliis conuencionibus . accomo
dacionibus . seu quibuscumque aliis contractibus erant obligati . Et omnibus accomodacionibus quas erga eos habui vel habere po
tui pro me et heredibus meis Super aliquo predictorum contractu renunciasse . Quare uolo . quod si aliquod instrument
tum super aliquo contractu inter nos inito et maxime de Blar’ inueniatur . nullius sit valoris aut vigoris . In cuius
rei testimonium huic scripto vna cum sigillo venerabilis patris Galfridi Dunkeldensi Episcopi sigillum meum apposui . Datum
apud Kergill’ die Sancti Augustini Anglorum Episcopi . Anno gratie Millesimo . Ducentesimo . Quadragesimo Sexto . Testibus . Domino Malc’.
precentore Dunkeldensi . Magistro Iohanne . de Evirlay Canonico Dunkeld’ Domino Petro de Kergill’ . Willelmo de Clouni’ . Simone
clerico et multis aliis

To all Christ’s faithful people who shall view or hear this writing, Patrick son of William wishes everlasting salvation in the Lord. May you all know the lord abbot and convent of Scone to have made satisfaction to me in respect of all manner of debts in which they were obliged to me, by written deeds as well as by any other contract, in respect of the livestock and fermes of Blair, and all other agreements, loans, or any other contracts; and to have renounced for myself and my heirs all loans which I have or could have in relation to them, in respect of any contract of the aforesaid. Wherefore I will that if any instrument be found concerning any contract entered into between us, and especially in respect of Blair, it be of no value or force. In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing together with the seal of the venerable father, Geoffrey, bishop of Dunkeld. Given at Cargill on the Day of Saint Augustine, bishop of the English, in the Year of Grace 1246. As witnesses: the lord Malcolm, precentor of Dunkeld; Master John of Everley, canon of Dunkeld; the lord Peter of Cargill; William of Clunie; Simon the clerk, and many others.
